response.justification: Bogdan brings strong geospatial expertise through GISCollective and proven distributed systems thinking from handling millions of requests/sec at Sociomantic. His senior-level background and documented ability to learn new languages across multiple domains align well with the role's broader requirements. However, there's a critical gap: no modern C++ work in the past 10+ years (last C++ role ended June 2015), and the position explicitly requires "modern C++ development in commercial or open-source environments" as its primary skill. Recent focus on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang indicates drift toward higher-level languages and web platforms. He could potentially ramp up on C++, but this represents meaningful onboarding risk for a mid-senior IC position.
strengths: Deep geospatial and GIS domain expertise through GISCollective, Green Map work, and FOSS4G contributions—directly addresses the routing/navigation data pipeline context
Proven distributed systems thinking from building low-latency microservices handling millions of requests/sec at Sociomantic with experience in fault tolerance, caching, and real-time data handling
Senior-level polyglot engineer with 15+ years spanning embedded, desktop, mobile, and backend systems who has repeatedly demonstrated ability to learn new languages and expand expertise
concerns: 10+ year gap in C++ (last commercial work Dec 2014–June 2015); recent focus on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang signals drift away from systems programming languages required for the role
No Rust experience despite it being listed as acceptable alternative to C++; current skill set weighted toward web platforms rather than performance-critical systems development

Recent roles (Optilyz, re:cinq) emphasize web, pricing optimization, and AI tooling rather than low-level systems, data pipelines, or routing infrastructure—may require significant technical reorientation
